challenges.title: Using calc() for Width
challenges.description: Write CSS that uses the `calc()` function to calculate widths. Create a rule for `.sidebar` that sets `width: calc(30% - 20px)`. Create a rule for `.main` that sets `width: calc(70% - 20px)`. Set `margin: 10px` on both. Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'calc(' in css.lower(), 'Should use calc() function'
assert '30%' in css, 'Should use 30% in sidebar width calculation'
assert '70%' in css, 'Should use 70% in main width calculation'
assert 'width' in css.lower(), 'Should set width property'
assert '.sidebar' in css.lower(), 'Should target .sidebar'
assert '.main' in css.lower(), 'Should target .main'
hints: calc() lets you mix units like percentages and pixels in a single expression
Always include spaces around + and - operators inside calc()

challenges.title: Combining CSS Functions
challenges.description: Write CSS that combines multiple CSS functions. Create a rule for `.responsive-box` that uses `width: clamp(200px, 50%, 800px)` for a responsive width, `padding: max(10px, 2vw)` for responsive padding, and `font-size: min(3vw, 24px)` for responsive text. Also add `margin: calc(1rem + 10px) auto`. Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'calc(' in css.lower(), 'Should use calc() function'
assert 'min(' in css.lower(), 'Should use min() function'
assert 'max(' in css.lower(), 'Should use max() function'
assert 'clamp(' in css.lower(), 'Should use clamp() function'
assert '.responsive-box' in css.lower(), 'Should target .responsive-box'
hints: clamp(min, preferred, max) constrains a value between a minimum and maximum
min() returns the smallest value; max() returns the largest value from the arguments
